source.page_label: Migratory Game Bird Seasons | Idaho Fish & Game
source.update_note: 2026-27 migratory bird data from IDFG. Idaho has 3 migratory bird areas and split between Pacific and Central Flyways.

species.name: Duck, Coot & Merganser
species.season_raw: Area 1: Oct 17-Jan 30; Area 2: Oct 3-Jan 15; Area 3: Oct 3-Jan 15

species.name: Goose (Canada & White-fronted)
species.season_raw: Area 1: Oct 17-Jan 30; Area 2: Oct 3-Jan 15; Area 3: Oct 3-Jan 15; Light: Nov 1-Feb 15

licenses.name: Resident Upland Game License
licenses.covers: Upland game, migratory birds, furbearers, turkey

licenses.name: Nonresident Upland Game License
licenses.covers: Migratory and upland birds for nonresidents

key_notes.title: Idaho has 3 migratory bird areas with different duck and goose season dates. Area 1 (northern) opens Oct 17; Areas 2&3 (southern) open Oct 3.

sources.label: IDFG — Migratory Bird Areas
sources.evidence: Area 1: Oct 17-Jan 30. Area 2: Oct 3-Jan 15. Area 3: Oct 3-Jan 15.

key_notes.title: Sandhill crane hunting in Idaho is limited to a single weekend (Sep 26-27) with a controlled hunt permit. Tag numbers were increased for 2026.

sources.label: IDFG — Sandhill Crane
sources.evidence: Sandhill Crane: weekend of Sept 27-28. Tags increased and additional seasons added.

key_notes.title: Mourning dove season runs Sep 1 - Oct 30 statewide (shorter than the federal framework limit). Eurasian collared-doves are year-round with no limit.

sources.label: IDFG — Dove Seasons
sources.evidence: Mourning & White-winged Dove: Sep 1 - Oct 30. Eurasian Collared: year-round, no limit.

key_notes.title: HIP certification is required for all migratory bird hunters and is included with the Upland Game License purchase.

sources.label: IDFG — HIP Requirement
sources.evidence: HIP permit required for all migratory bird hunters.
